findings.title: Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20127 Auth Bypass — Zero-Day Since 2023, CISA Emergency Directive 26-03
findings.summary: Critical authentication bypass in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller exploited as a zero-day since at least 2023, now added to CISA KEV with Emergency Directive 26-03 requiring immediate federal agency remediation.

findings.title: UNC2814 GRIDTIDE — Chinese Espionage Campaign Using Google Sheets C2 Hits 53 Orgs in 42 Countries
findings.summary: Google/Mandiant disrupted a Chinese state-sponsored espionage campaign (UNC2814) deploying the GRIDTIDE backdoor that abuses Google Sheets API for C2 communications, impacting 53 organizations across 42 countries primarily targeting telecoms and government agencies.

findings.title: US Sanctions Operation Zero — Russian Exploit Broker Caught Buying Stolen L3Harris Zero-Days
findings.summary: US Treasury sanctioned Russian exploit broker Operation Zero and owner Sergey Zelenyuk under PAIPA for purchasing 8 stolen zero-day exploits from former L3Harris executive Peter Williams, who was sentenced to 87 months in prison.

findings.title: Fake Next.js Job Interview Malware Campaign Targets Software Developers
findings.summary: Microsoft Defender team identified a coordinated campaign using malicious Next.js repositories disguised as coding assessments and job interview tests that achieve RCE on developer machines through VS Code folder-open triggers, trojanized dev servers, and backend startup hooks.

findings.title: Zyxel Critical RCE in Consumer Routers — 12+ Models Affected
findings.summary: Zyxel issued an advisory for a critical remote code execution vulnerability affecting over a dozen consumer router models, with no patch available for some end-of-life devices.

findings.title: SolarWinds Patches Four Critical Serv-U Vulnerabilities
findings.summary: SolarWinds released patches for four new critical vulnerabilities in Serv-U file transfer software, following the previous CVE-2025-40538 which was actively exploited and added to CISA KEV.

findings.title: Lazarus Group Confirmed Working With Medusa Ransomware — Symantec Analysis
findings.summary: Symantec/Broadcom published detailed analysis confirming North Korean Lazarus Group is now actively collaborating with Medusa ransomware operators, expanding the threat beyond previously reported initial indicators.

noted.summary: Legacy Cisco SD-WAN path traversal vulnerability added to CISA KEV alongside CVE-2026-20127 as part of Emergency Directive 26-03.
Chromium RCE being exploited in the wild per avleonov.com Linux Patch Wednesday analysis.
Microsoft Office RCE trending in vulnerability management per Positive Technologies February digest.
Schneier on Security highlighted trivial poisoning of AI training data via personal websites affecting Google Gemini and ChatGPT within 24 hours.
